headline: Ukraine CONFIRM at 50.0 Functional on day 2 of US-brokered trilateral ceasefire — score held pending day 3 close (May 11). Day-2 evidence is consistent with the May 9 day-1 conditional credit posture: Ukraine's own conduct remains breach-free; the 1,000-prisoner exchange list was formally submitted (concrete ACT/INT signal); Russian-side violations (3 civilians killed, 19 injured by Russian strikes; ~210 battlefield clashes; ~10,000 drone strikes per Zelenskyy 48-hour aggregate) register against Russia as counterparty under the inverted-signal attribution rule applied evidence-by-evidence (May 9 methodology call carried forward). Per the prompt's explicit instruction: hold scoring open until day-3 evidence (May 11) and prisoner exchange outcome. No new additive scoring events above the May 9 baseline. The trilateral-format conditional-credit window remains open. Note Russia's day-2 prisoner-exchange blame-shifting (Putin claim that Ukraine 'not ready' for 500-name swap) DOES NOT invert attribution: Ukraine submitted the agreed 1,000-name list publicly on May 11, Zelenskyy stated the exchange 'must take place,' and 'Americans assumed responsibility for guarantees' — Ukraine's posture is cooperative-and-on-record. Score held at 50.0 with explicit 'pending day 3 close' status note.
ceasefire_window_status: day 2 of 3 — Ukraine maintains breach-free posture; full window scoring outcome deferred to May 11 day-3 evidence
key_evidence: May 10 2026 (in-window): Day 2 of trilateral US-brokered ceasefire (May 9-11). Russia conducted strikes killing 3 civilians and injuring 19 across Ukraine (Kharkiv, Kherson); ~210 battlefield clashes along 1,200km front line; Ukrainian forces maintained defensive posture without attributable ceasefire-breach conduct.

May 10-11 2026 (in-window): Ukraine submitted the 1,000-prisoner exchange list publicly per Zelenskyy. Zelenskyy: exchange 'must take place — Americans assumed responsibility for guarantees.' Concrete day-2 ACT signal of cooperative follow-through despite Russian violations.

May 10 2026 (in-window): Russia's MoD claimed '16,071 Ukrainian violations' while conducting documented strikes — mutual-blame frame attempt continues from day 1; ISW and independent reporting confirm Russian-attributed civilian harm pattern.

May 10 2026 (in-window): Putin accused Ukraine of 'delaying' prisoner exchange (claimed Russia sent 500-name list May 5; Ukraine asked for time and proposed ~200; Russia says Ukraine later said 'not ready'). Blame-shifting on most visible ACT signal — Ukraine response: public submission of 1,000-name list per agreed framework.

May 10 2026 (in-window, Zelenskyy aggregate for May 9-10): Russia conducted 150+ ground assaults, ~100 artillery strikes, ~10,000 drone strikes — front-line operations at roughly normal tempo; only large-scale strategic bombardments reduced (the same partial-compliance pattern noted on day 1).

Day-1 conditional credit (50.0) granted May 9 under trilateral methodology call; day-2 evidence sustains the conditional credit but does not yet warrant additive scoring above the day-1 baseline — final ceasefire-window scoring outcome deferred to day 3 (May 11).

evidence.source: Kyiv Independent — Russia breaks 3-day ceasefire as strikes kill 1 injure 19
evidence.finding: Day 2 documented Russian civilian-harm strikes (1+ killed, 19 injured) during agreed ceasefire window. Ukrainian-side civilian-protection and reporting infrastructure functional.

evidence.source: Al Jazeera — Russia kills three Ukrainians in 24 hours accuses Kyiv of violating truce
evidence.finding: Three civilians killed on day 2; mutual-blame frame attempt by Russia (16,071 alleged Ukrainian violations); independent reporting confirms attribution.

evidence.source: US News — Ukraine reports battlefield clashes drone strikes despite ceasefire
evidence.finding: Ukrainian General Staff reported ~210 clashes along 1,200km front. Ukrainian transparency and operational reporting sustained; balanced public reporting of partial-compliance pattern.

evidence.source: PBS Newshour — Russia and Ukraine trade blame for continued fighting
evidence.finding: Multi-source confirmation of day-2 conduct pattern; ceasefire nearing day-3 close; 1,000-prisoner exchange remains highest-signal forward event.

evidence.source: Critical Threats — Russian offensive campaign assessment May 10 2026
evidence.finding: ISW: Russian offensive operations continued at roughly normal operational tempo across 210+ clashes on day 2; partial-compliance pattern entrenched. Ukrainian defensive posture maintained.

evidence.source: Ukrinform — Zelensky says 1000-for-1000 prisoner exchange must take place
evidence.finding: Ukraine submitted 1,000-prisoner exchange list. Zelenskyy public commitment: 'must take place — Americans assumed responsibility for guarantees.' Concrete day-2 ACT/INT follow-through despite Russian breach pattern.

evidence.source: Euromaidan Press — Talks continue on Trump-brokered prisoner exchange after Putin claimed Kyiv was not ready
evidence.finding: Putin's 'not ready' framing publicly contested by Ukrainian list submission; Ukrainian-side cooperative posture documented in real time.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained. A1 detection: Ukrainian General Staff and civil-defense reporting infrastructure documented all day-2 strikes within hours; A4 amplification: balanced Zelenskyy public communications acknowledged both partial Russian compliance AND continued strikes (no propaganda-only posture); no AWR-degrading conduct in window.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained. E1 affective resonance: state acknowledgment of Russian-caused civilian harm (3 killed, 19 injured day 2) within hours; no E1-degrading conduct attributable to Ukraine; civilian-protection posture maintained.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.5 sustained. AC1 responsiveness: 1,000-prisoner exchange list publicly submitted on day 2-3 boundary = concrete cooperative-action follow-through ON SCHEDULE; AC2 proportionality: ceasefire-engagement preserved despite documented Russian breaches; defensive posture maintained without attributable Ukrainian breach. Hold at 3.5 (sub-threshold from 4.0 upgrade pending day-3 outcome of prisoner exchange completion).
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained. No EQ-relevant changes in window; structural pattern from prior cycles.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 sustained. B3 scope clarity: ceasefire-engagement framing maintained civilian-protection boundary across day 2; B4 refusal ethics: Ukraine did not invoke disproportionate retaliation despite documented Russian civilian harm.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained. AB2 transparency: balanced Zelenskyy reporting on day 2 (Russian reduction + Russian violations + Ukrainian compliance status) — accountability posture sustained; AB3 transparency: General Staff clash counts publicly accessible; AB1 harm-acknowledgment: civilian casualty disclosure within hours.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained. S5 coalitional compassion: continued participation in US-brokered trilateral structure across day 2; S2 long-term impact: 1,000-prisoner exchange list submission is a structural cooperative-action commitment beyond a single-cycle gesture; preparation for day-3 outcome.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained. I1 consistency under pressure: Ukraine maintained ceasefire engagement and prisoner-swap follow-through despite Russian breach pattern across two days; declared civilian-protection commitment sustained; the bad-faith-ceasefire inverted-signal rule applied evidence-by-evidence keeps the breach cost on Russia; Ukraine's day-2 posture earns conditional credit toward full INT upgrade if day-3 sustains.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 50.0. Sub-threshold drift (0.0). Functional band sustained. Score held with explicit 'pending day 3 close' status note.

trilateral_methodology_call: DAY-2 EVIDENCE CONSISTENT WITH DAY-1 CONDITIONAL CREDIT. The May 9 trilateral-format methodology call established that (1) the trilateral format is structurally distinct from May 5-7 unilateral declarations, (2) the inverted-signal attribution rule applies evidence-by-evidence not format-wide, and (3) full credit for either party is available conditional on sustained compliance over days 2-3. Day-2 evidence sustains the day-1 conditional posture: Ukraine breach-free + cooperative follow-through (prisoner list submission); Russia accumulating breach evidence (3 civilians killed, 19 injured, 210 clashes, blame-shifting rhetoric). Ukraine's conditional credit is preserved. Full-credit upgrade decision deferred to day 3 (May 11) with prisoner exchange completion as the highest-signal forward event.
negativeSignals: Active war context; civilian casualties from Russian Kharkiv/Kherson strikes on day 2; partial-compliance pattern leaves uncertainty over day-3 sustainability; Russian rhetorical accusations (16,071 alleged violations) attempt to construct mutual-blame frame; Russia's blame-shifting on prisoner exchange introduces a forward-event-failure risk that could yet collapse the cooperative trajectory.
positiveSignals: US-brokered trilateral format sustains structural distinction from collapsed unilateral declarations; both presidents remained publicly committed at day 2 close; 1,000-prisoner exchange list publicly submitted by Ukraine = concrete cooperative-action follow-through; Ukrainian civil-defense and balanced public-reporting infrastructure functional; Zelenskyy's 'must take place' framing publicly rebuts Putin's 'not ready' blame-shift; per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire-rule inversion preserved — Russia bears violation cost.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / active-conflict cluster. Day-2 of the trilateral ceasefire window. Cross-reference: Russia (paired counterparty, floor sustained), Belarus (counterparty-adjacent), United States (third-party broker — separate scoring entity). The trilateral format's day-2 evidence reinforces the May 9 methodology precedent: evidence-driven attribution is robust across multiple days under partial-compliance conditions.
open_actions_resolved: Day 2 trilateral-ceasefire conduct documented for Ukraine
Day-1 conditional credit posture preserved on day-2 evidence
Ukrainian-side conduct in window does not invert bad-faith-ceasefire rule
1,000-prisoner exchange list submission documented as concrete cooperative-action signal
Putin's 'not ready' blame-shifting attempt did not invert attribution (Ukraine response was public list submission)
open_actions_carried: May 11 EMERGENCY re-queue: day 3 of trilateral ceasefire — final scoring day; prisoner exchange completion or failure is highest-signal ACT event
Watch: any Ukrainian-side conduct on day 3 that would invert attribution rule
Watch: post-ceasefire posture (sustained or collapsed pattern determines INT trajectory and possible upgrade)

Conditional ACT/INT upgrade path: if exchange completes and Ukrainian conduct remains breach-free across day 3, ACT and INT subdimensions become candidates for +0.5 each in the day-3 final scoring pass (would raise composite by ~3 points)
methodology_consistency_check: Day-2 assessment uses the same evidence-by-evidence attribution rule applied on May 9. Russia's day-2 breaches (3 civilians killed, 210 clashes) register against Russia, not Ukraine. Ukraine's day-2 conduct (no attributable breaches, prisoner list submission) earns conditional credit. The day-2 call is identical in structure to day 1 — confirmed methodologically consistent.
